title: scilla non-scripta subsp. cernua
intro: Scilla non-scripta subsp. cernua, also known as the hanging bluebell or nodding bluebell, is a subspecies of the wild bluebell (Scilla non-scripta) plant, characterized by its drooping bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Scilla non-scripta subsp. cernua include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and relieve congestion, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
